# FM-R.1 — Full Moon Results Page Redesign Audit
**Date:** 2026-05-10
**Mode:** Audit-only (read-only). No source files modified.
**Scope:** Inspection and design phase before implementing role-based redesign (FM-R.2).
**Pre-flight:** `git status` shows only `public/sitemap.xml` modified (auto-generated by preceding `build:full`). No source modifications. Branch: `main`. Up to date with origin.

---

## 1. Visual Design System Inventory

### 1A. Global CSS (`src/index.css`)

No CSS custom properties are defined locally. The file imports Tailwind CSS and two Google Fonts:

```css
@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Playfair+Display:wght@400;700&family=Roboto:wght@400;500;700&display=swap');
@import "tailwindcss";
```

**Typography:**
- `h1`, `h2`, `h3`: `font-family: 'Playfair Display', Georgia, serif` (global rule, lines 9–11)
- All other elements: `font-family: 'Roboto', system-ui, sans-serif` (body, line 5)
- `body { background-color: #ffffff }` (line 7)

**Border-radius baseline:** `button, input, textarea, select { border-radius: 4px }` (lines 13–16)

**CSS custom properties in use:** All come from `mm-design` via `var(--mm-color-blue)`, `var(--mm-color-red)`, `var(--mm-color-green)`, `var(--mm-color-yellow)`, `var(--mm-color-black)`, `var(--mm-color-gray-100)`, `var(--mm-color-gray-50)`, `var(--mm-color-blue-tint)`. These are set by the mm-design CSS files imported at runtime — they are not redefined in `src/index.css`.

**No Tailwind config file** (`tailwind.config.js`) exists at the repo root. Tailwind v4 is used directly via `@import "tailwindcss"`. All custom theming is expressed through inline `style={}` props using mm-design token values (hex strings or CSS variable references).

---

### 1B. Design tokens (`src/design/tokens.js` → `mm-design/tokens/index.js`)

The file is a pure re-export shim. All tokens come from `mm-design`. Actual values from `node_modules/mm-design/tokens/`:

**Brand palette (hex, `colors.*`):**
| Token | Hex | Usage |
|---|---|---|
| `colors.red` | `#cf3339` | RoleCard accent, primary role bar, danger button, `error` |
| `colors.blue` | `#0047ba` | Primary buttons, nav header, witness score tick, `primary` |
| `colors.yellow` | `#f1c22f` | Beta badge, `warning` |
| `colors.green` | `#427c42` | Free badge, `success` |
| `colors.white` | `#ffffff` | – |
| `colors.black` | `#111111` | – |

**Semantic aliases (`colors.*`):**
| Token | Value | Usage |
|---|---|---|
| `colors.primary` | `#cf3339` | Same as red |
| `colors.primaryDark` | `#a8262b` | – |
| `colors.background` | `#ffffff` | – |
| `colors.surface` | `#ffffff` | – |
| `colors.border` | `#d4d4d4` | Non-primary probability bars |
| `colors.textPrimary` | `#111111` | Headings, labels, primary text |
| `colors.textMuted` | `#666666` | Notes, descriptions, muted labels |
| `colors.success` | `#427c42` | – |
| `colors.warning` | `#f1c22f` | – |
| `colors.error` | `#cf3339` | – |

**Report UI tokens:**
| Token | Usage |
|---|---|
| `colors.arcBar` | Arc role probability bar color |
| `colors.arcLabel` | Arc role name color |
| `colors.selfBar` | Self score bar in DimensionRow |
| `colors.trackBg` | Bar track background |

**Role colors (`ROLE_COLORS`, R01–R12):**
| Role | Name | Color |
|---|---|---|
| R01 | Dolphin | `#cf3339` (red) |
| R02 | Wolf | `#d46010` (orange) |
| R03 | Elephant | `#c08800` (dark yellow) |
| R04 | Owl | `#6a8800` (olive) |
| R05 | Eagle | `#427c42` (green) |
| R06 | Falcon | `#1a8c60` (teal-green) |
| R07 | Octopus | `#0d8888` (teal) |
| R08 | Tortoise | `#1a68b0` (medium blue) |
| R09 | Bee | `#3355c8` (blue) |
| R10 | Bear | `#6630a8` (purple) |
| R11 | Fox | `#a030a0` (magenta) |
| R12 | Badger | `#b03070` (crimson-pink) |

These 12 colors span the full hue wheel from red through orange, yellow, green, teal, blue, purple to crimson. **No two roles share the same hue.** This is the intended system for role-specific coloring.

**Domain colors (`DOMAIN_COLORS`, `DOMAIN_ICON_CLASSES`, `DOMAIN_BG_CLASSES`):**
| Domain | Color | Icon class | BG class |
|---|---|---|---|
| presence | (extraversion red variant) | `text-amber-400` | `bg-amber-400` |
| bond | (agreeableness green variant) | `text-emerald-500` | `bg-emerald-500` |
| vision | `#427c42` (brand green) | `text-[#427c42]` | `bg-[#427c42 tint]` |
| discipline | (blue variant) | `text-blue-600` | `bg-blue-600` |
| depth | (red variant) | `text-red-500` | `bg-red-500` |

**Spacing tokens (`spacing.*`):**
- `spacing.pageMax`: `720px`

**Radius:** `sm: 2px`, `base: 4px`, `lg: 12px`, `full: 9999px`

**Breakpoints:** `sm: 640px`, `md: 768px`, `lg: 1024px`, `xl: 1280px`

**Page max-width:** `max-w-4xl` (56rem / 896px) as set in `src/components/Layout.jsx` line 297.

---

### 1C. UI Components (`src/components/ui/`)

#### `Card` (Card.jsx)
- **Props:** `accent` (`none|red|green|yellow|blue`), `className`
- **Default (no accent):** `bg-white border border-gray-200 rounded`
- **Accent:** `bg-white rounded` + inline `borderStyle: solid, borderWidth: '0 0 0 3px', borderColor: ACCENT_COLORS[accent]`
- **Radius:** `rounded` = Tailwind default 4px (matching mm-design base radius)
- **Used in:** Every section of FullMoonResultsPage. `RoleCard` uses `Card accent="red"`. Most other cards use no accent.

#### `Button` (Button.jsx)
- **Props:** `variant` (`primary|secondary|ghost|danger`), `size` (`sm|md|lg`), standard button props
- **Primary:** `bg-[var(--mm-color-blue)] text-white hover:opacity-90` (blue fill)
- **Danger:** `bg-[var(--mm-color-red)] text-white hover:opacity-90` (red fill)
- **Shared:** `font-semibold inline-flex items-center justify-center transition-colors rounded`
- **Size md (default):** `text-sm px-5 py-2.5`
- **Used in:** Share button, Invite witnesses CTA, retake button

#### `Badge` (Badge.jsx)
- **Props:** `variant` (`default|beta|paid|free`), `className`
- **All variants:** `inline-flex items-center text-xs font-semibold px-2.5 py-1 rounded`
- **Default:** `bg-gray-100 text-gray-700`
- **Beta:** `bg-[var(--mm-color-yellow)] text-[var(--mm-color-black)]`
- **Paid:** `bg-[var(--mm-color-blue)] text-white`
- **Free:** `bg-[var(--mm-color-green)] text-white`
- **Used in:** RoleCard (beta badge or "Witness included" blue badge), arc role tags

#### `SectionLabel` (SectionLabel.jsx)
- **Props:** `color` (`blue|red|green|amber|gray`), `className`
- **All:** `text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-widest`
- **Colors:** maps to brand CSS vars or Tailwind classes
- **Used in:** Every section eyebrow. Gray is by far the most common on the Full Moon page.

---

### 1D. Report Components (`src/components/report/`)

#### `RoleCard` (RoleCard.jsx)
- **Props:** `role`, `roleName`, `roleEssence`, `arc[]`, `arcName(key)`, `arcLabel`, `badge` (JSX), `badgeNote`
- **Visual:** `Card accent="red"` → left red 3px border. `flex flex-row`.
- **Left column:** `w-40 shrink-0` containing `<RoleIcon role={role} size={128} style={{ color: colors.red }}>`
- **Right column:** `flex-1 p-6 sm:p-8` containing badge (if any), `<h2 className="text-5xl sm:text-6xl font-bold">` (role name in Playfair), essence text (`text-base leading-relaxed`), arc badges.
- **Arc row:** `text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-widest` label + `flex flex-wrap gap-2` of `<Badge variant="default">` per arc role

This is the visual hero of the page. It dominates Section 1 with the large icon (128px) and Playfair Display at `text-5xl sm:text-6xl`.

#### `RadarDataCard` (RadarDataCard.jsx)
- **Props:** `scores`, `domainKeys`, `labelFn`, `customFirstCol`, `children` (1 or 2 children)
- **Visual:** `Card className="shadow-sm p-5"` containing a `grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2` (1 child) or `md:grid-cols-3` (2 children)
- **Column 1:** `RadarChart` or `customFirstCol`
- **Columns 2–3:** each `div className="flex flex-col gap-0"` wrapping a child

#### `DimensionRow` (DimensionRow.jsx)
- **Props:** `domainKey`, `domainName`, `score`, `pct`, `labelTier`, `labelText`, `description`, `witnessScore`, `witnessPct`, `compact`, `maxScore`
- **Standard mode:** icon (14px) + name (`text-sm font-semibold`) + score (`text-sm font-bold tabular-nums`) + witness score (blue) + tier badge + filled progress bar (h-1.5 rounded-full, 500ms transition) + optional witness tick (blue vertical line at witnessPct%)
- **Compact mode:** smaller, used in Last Quarter

#### `FacetAccordion` (FacetAccordion.jsx)
- **Visual:** `flex flex-col gap-2` of Cards. Each Card: clickable header (domain dot + name + facet count + ChevronRight that rotates 90° when expanded) + collapsible `grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 gap-x-6 gap-y-4` of facet bars
- **Facet bars:** h-1 bg-gray-100, colored fill using `barHex + '22'` (22% alpha) for badge background
- **Print:** `.facet-accordion-panel` CSS class forces panels open

#### `ReportPageHeader` (ReportPageHeader.jsx)
- **Props:** `icon`, `eyebrow`, `title`, `subtitle`
- **Visual:** icon + eyebrow (`text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-widest`) + `h1 text-2xl sm:text-3xl font-bold` (Playfair) + `text-sm` subtitle

#### `ConvergenceMeter` (ConvergenceMeter.jsx) ← **TO BE DELETED in FM-R.2**
- Current Ruta 2 implementation: accepts `rho` and `comparison`, renders qualitative label + 5-row rank table
- The entire file will be removed; the import removed from `report/index.js` and `FullMoonResultsPage.jsx`

---

### 1E. FullMoonResultsPage vertical rhythm and color usage

**Page container:** `<main className="py-10 sm:py-16">` → `<div className="flex flex-col gap-8">`. All sections separated by `gap-8` (2rem).

**Section pattern:** `<section>` → `<SectionLabel color="gray">` (or sometimes inline eyebrow) → content card(s).

**Typography hierarchy on the page:**
1. Page title (`h1`): `text-2xl sm:text-3xl` Playfair Display — "Full Moon report"
2. Role name (`h2`): `text-5xl sm:text-6xl` Playfair Display — inside RoleCard
3. Section eyebrows: `text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-widest` gray — SectionLabel
4. Domain names: `text-sm font-semibold` — in DimensionRow
5. Body text: `text-sm leading-relaxed` — descriptions, notes
6. Muted notes: `text-xs` — witness count, bar percentages, disclaimer

**Brand color usage by section:**
- **Red** (#cf3339): RoleCard left accent border; RoleIcon color (`style={{ color: colors.red }}`); primary role probability bar. Red signals the core identity.
- **Blue** (#0047ba): "Witness included" badge (`bg-blue-100 text-blue-700`); witness score number in DimensionRow; witness tick mark on bars; primary Button (CTA); nav header.
- **Yellow** (#f1c22f): Beta badge (solo result, before witnesses).
- **Green** (#427c42): "Done" status badge for completed witnesses (`text-emerald-600 bg-emerald-50`); ConvergenceMeter "strong" state.
- **Gray (Tailwind)**: Almost everything structural — SectionLabels, borders, muted text.

---

## 2. Animal Icon Inventory

### 2A. Source
All 12 animal icons live in `mm-design/icons/animals/` (installed as npm dependency). They are imported in `mm-design/icons/react/MoonIcons.jsx` as raw SVG strings via Vite's `?raw` import. Re-exported through `src/components/MoonIcons.jsx` (pure shim).

### 2B. Per-role inventory

| Role | Animal | Source file | Format |
|---|---|---|---|
| R01 | Dolphin | `cercol-icon-r01-dolphin.svg` | potrace fill SVG |
| R02 | Wolf | `cercol-icon-r02-wolf.svg` | potrace fill SVG |
| R03 | Elephant | `cercol-icon-r03-elephant.svg` | potrace fill SVG |
| R04 | Owl | `cercol-icon-r04-owl.svg` | potrace fill SVG |
| R05 | Eagle | `cercol-icon-r05-eagle.svg` | potrace fill SVG |
| R06 | Falcon | `cercol-icon-r06-falcon.svg` | potrace fill SVG |
| R07 | Octopus | `cercol-icon-r07-octopus.svg` | potrace fill SVG |
| R08 | Tortoise | `cercol-icon-r08-tortoise.svg` | potrace fill SVG |
| R09 | Bee | `cercol-icon-r09-bee.svg` | potrace fill SVG |
| R10 | Bear | `cercol-icon-r10-bear.svg` | potrace fill SVG |
| R11 | Fox | `cercol-icon-r11-fox.svg` | potrace fill SVG |
| R12 | Badger | `cercol-icon-r12-badger.svg` | potrace fill SVG |

All 12 are present. No missing icons.

**Format details:** Each is a potrace-generated fill SVG (`fill="currentColor"`, no fixed `width`/`height`). The `prepareAnimalSvg()` function (MoonIcons.jsx line 473) extracts `viewBox` and inner `<g>` content at module load time and renders them as inline SVGs via `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`. This means the icons inherit `currentColor` from their parent and accept `size`, `className`, `style` props exactly like all other icons.

**Default size:** 32px (`size = 32` in each component).

**Color:** Monochrome. Controlled entirely by `currentColor`. The caller sets the color via `style={{ color: ... }}` or `className="text-..."`.

### 2C. Current rendering locations

| Location | Icon | Size | Color source |
|---|---|---|---|
| `RoleCard` (Section 1 of FullMoonResultsPage) | `<RoleIcon role={roleResult.role} size={128} style={{ color: colors.red }}>` | 128px | `colors.red` (#cf3339) hardcoded |
| `RoleProbabilityBars` (Section 2) | `<RoleIcon role={r} size={16} style={{ flexShrink: 0 }}>` | 16px | Inherits page color (black/current) |

**Pattern:** Large icon in the hero card (128px, red), small icon in probability rows (16px, neutral). The new role-comparison view will use an intermediate size (proposed: 20px) to be scannable without dominating.

### 2D. `RoleIcon` wrapper
```jsx
export function RoleIcon({ role, size = 32, className = '', style }) {
  const Icon = ROLE_ICON_MAP[role]
  return Icon ? <Icon size={size} className={className} style={style} /> : null
}
```
`ROLE_ICON_MAP` (MoonIcons.jsx line 566) maps `R01`–`R12` to the corresponding animal component. Any unknown role key silently renders `null`.

---

## 3. Role Centroids and Top Adjectives

### 3A. Centroids (`src/utils/role-scoring.js`)

Centroid order: `[E, A, O, C, N]` = `[presence, bond, vision, discipline, depth]` (z-scores).

| Role | Animal | E | A | O | C | N |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| R01 | Dolphin | +1.0 | +1.0 | 0.0 | 0.0 | −0.5 |
| R02 | Wolf | +1.0 | −1.0 | 0.0 | +0.5 | +0.3 |
| R03 | Elephant | −1.0 | +1.0 | 0.0 | 0.0 | −0.8 |
| R04 | Owl | −1.0 | −1.0 | 0.0 | +0.8 | −0.5 |
| R05 | Eagle | +1.0 | 0.0 | +1.0 | −0.3 | −0.5 |
| R06 | Falcon | +1.0 | 0.0 | −1.0 | +0.8 | −0.3 |
| R07 | Octopus | −1.0 | 0.0 | +1.0 | −0.8 | 0.0 |
| R08 | Tortoise | −1.0 | 0.0 | −1.0 | +1.0 | −0.8 |
| R09 | Bee | 0.0 | +1.0 | +1.0 | +0.8 | −0.5 |
| R10 | Bear | 0.0 | +1.0 | −1.0 | +0.5 | −0.8 |
| R11 | Fox | 0.0 | −1.0 | +1.0 | −0.8 | +0.3 |
| R12 | Badger | 0.0 | −1.0 | −1.0 | +0.8 | −0.3 |

**Probability formula:** Euclidean distance in 5D z-space to each centroid → softmax over negated distances → primary role = minimum distance, arc = probability > 0.15 (excluding primary). (`role-scoring.js` lines 61–103)

### 3B. Witness adjective corpus (`src/data/witness-adjectives.js`)

- **100 entries** confirmed (lines 22–141)
- **Schema confirmed:** `{id, en, ca, factor, valence, tip: {en, ca}}`
- **Distribution:** 20 per factor (E, A, O, C, N); exactly 10 positive (+1) and 10 negative (−1) valence per factor
- **ID format:** `{factor}{sign}{nn}` e.g. `E+01`, `N-05`

### 3C. Fit score computation

For each adjective `adj` and role centroid `R`:
```
fit(adj, R) = z_R[adj.factor] * sign(adj.valence)
```
Where `sign(valence) = +1` if `valence > 0`, `−1` if `valence < 0`, and `z_R[factor]` is the centroid z-score for the adjective's OCEAN factor.

All adjectives within a factor group receive the same fit score (since each group has one z-score per role). Tiebreaking: first by id order (E+01 before E+02, etc.), but representative picks below are chosen for semantic distinctiveness.

### 3D. Top 5 adjectives per role

| Role | #1 EN (CA) | #2 EN (CA) | #3 EN (CA) | #4 EN (CA) | #5 EN (CA) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **R01 Dolphin** | outgoing (obert) | enthusiastic (entusiasta) | warm (càlid) | empathetic (empàtic) | composed (serè) |
| **R02 Wolf** | outgoing (obert) | assertive (assertiu) | critical (crític) | demanding (exigent) | organized (organitzat) |
| **R03 Elephant** | reserved (reservat) | quiet (callat) | warm (càlid) | caring (atent) | stable (estable) |
| **R04 Owl** | reserved (reservat) | critical (crític) | organized (organitzat) | methodical (metòdic) | composed (serè) |
| **R05 Eagle** | outgoing (obert) | enthusiastic (entusiasta) | creative (creatiu) | curious (curiós) | stable (estable) |
| **R06 Falcon** | outgoing (obert) | assertive (assertiu) | organized (organitzat) | structured (estructurat) | straightforward (directe) |
| **R07 Octopus** | reserved (reservat) | solitary (solitari) | creative (creatiu) | imaginative (imaginatiu) | scattered (dispersat) |
| **R08 Tortoise** | organized (organitzat) | diligent (diligent) | reliable (fiable) | thorough (exhaustiu) | stable (estable) |
| **R09 Bee** | warm (càlid) | empathetic (empàtic) | creative (creatiu) | curious (curiós) | organized (organitzat) |
| **R10 Bear** | warm (càlid) | caring (atent) | stable (estable) | composed (serè) | traditional (tradicional) |
| **R11 Fox** | critical (crític) | creative (creatiu) | curious (curiós) | scattered (dispersat) | irritable (irritable) |
| **R12 Badger** | demanding (exigent) | critical (crític) | organized (organitzat) | methodical (metòdic) | structured (estructurat) |

**Fit score breakdown by role (top groups):**
- Dolphin: E+(1.0), A+(1.0), N−(0.5)
- Wolf: E+(1.0), A−(1.0), C+(0.5), N+(0.3)
- Elephant: E−(1.0), A+(1.0), N−(0.8)
- Owl: E−(1.0), A−(1.0), C+(0.8), N−(0.5)
- Eagle: E+(1.0), O+(1.0), N−(0.5), C−(0.3)
- Falcon: E+(1.0), O−(1.0), C+(0.8), N−(0.3)
- Octopus: E−(1.0), O+(1.0), C−(0.8)
- Tortoise: E−(1.0), O−(1.0), C+(1.0), N−(0.8)
- Bee: A+(1.0), O+(1.0), C+(0.8), N−(0.5)
- Bear: A+(1.0), O−(1.0), N−(0.8), C+(0.5)
- Fox: A−(1.0), O+(1.0), C−(0.8), N+(0.3)
- Badger: A−(1.0), O−(1.0), C+(0.8), N−(0.3)

These adjective lists will become the `ROLE_TOP_ADJECTIVES` exported constant in FM-R.2.

## 4. Data Inventory for the Redesign

### 4A. Available derived values in `FullMoonResultsPage.jsx`

| Variable | Line | Value |
|---|---|---|
| `domains` | 68 | `{presence, bond, vision, discipline, depth}` on 1–5 (self scores) |
| `selfRole` | 136 | `computeRole(domains)` → `{role, arc, probabilities}` |
| `completedSessions` | 138 | `sessions.filter(s => s.completed_at && s.scores)` |
| `hasEnoughWitnesses` | 140 | `completedSessions.length >= 2` |
| `hasAnyWitness` | 141 | `completedSessions.length >= 1` |
| `witnessScores` | 143 | `averageWitnessScores(...)` or `null` |
| `witnessRole` | 146 | `computeRole(witnessScores)` or `null` |
| `roleResult` | 149 | `computeCombinedRole(selfRole, witnessRole)` |
| `t` | 42 | `useTranslation()` i18n function |

The page has full access to `selfRole.probabilities`, `witnessRole.probabilities` (when witnesses exist), individual session data, and the i18n function. No additional API calls are needed for the new block.

### 4B. Section 4 and 5 exact line ranges (post-Ruta-2 merge, commit 42cb572)

**Section 4 (ConvergenceMeter) — lines 282–290:**
```jsx
{/* ── Section 4: Convergence meter (≥2 witnesses) ── */}
{hasEnoughWitnesses && rho !== null && (
  <section>
    <SectionLabel color="gray" className="mb-4">
      {t('witnessResults.convergenceSection')}
    </SectionLabel>
    <ConvergenceMeter rho={rho} comparison={rankComparison} t={t} />
  </section>
)}
```

**Section 5 (Blind spots) — lines 292–334:**
```jsx
{/* ── Section 5: Blind spots (≥2 witnesses) ── */}
{hasEnoughWitnesses && (
  <section>
    <SectionLabel color="gray" className="mb-1 flex items-center gap-1.5">
      <BlindSpotsIcon size={16} />{t('witnessResults.blindSpotsTitle')}
    </SectionLabel>
    <p className="text-xs mb-4" style={{ color: colors.textMuted }}>
      {t('witnessResults.blindSpotsNote')}
    </p>
    {divergence.length === 0 ? (
      <Card className="px-5 py-4">
        <p className="text-sm" style={{ color: colors.textMuted }}>
          {t('witnessResults.noDivergence')}
        </p>
      </Card>
    ) : (
      <Card className="px-5 py-4">
        <ul className="flex flex-col gap-2.5">
          {divergence.map(({ domain, selfScore, witnessScore }) => {
            const direction  = witnessScore > selfScore ? 'witnessHigher' : 'selfHigher'
            const desc       = t(`witnessResults.blindSpots.${domain}.${direction}`)
            ...
          })}
        </ul>
      </Card>
    )}
  </section>
)}
```

Both sections gated on `hasEnoughWitnesses`. The FM-R.2 replacement block will occupy these same lines.

---

## 5. Visual Design of the New Block

### 5A. Section header

**Proposed text:** `"How you and your witnesses see you"` → i18n key `witnessResults.roleViewSection`

**Alternatives:**
- `"Two perspectives"` (shorter, more poetic)
- `"Role comparison"` (clinical but clear)

**Rendered as:** `<SectionLabel color="gray">` — same eyebrow style as every other section. No special color. Red would over-emphasize; blue has a "witness data" connotation already established.

---

### 5B. Side-by-side role view

**Container:** `Card className="shadow-sm p-5"` (matches RadarDataCard container)

**Grid:** `grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2` with a visible divider between columns on sm+. On mobile: self column on top, separator (`border-t border-gray-100 my-4`), witness column below.

**Column sub-headers:** `text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-widest text-gray-400` — this exactly matches `SectionLabel color="gray"` rendered inline. Text:
- Left: `"YOU SEE YOURSELF AS"` → i18n key `witnessResults.selfViewLabel`
- Right: `"WITNESSES SEE YOU AS"` → i18n key `witnessResults.witnessViewLabel`

**Each role row:**
```
[RoleIcon 20px] [Role name text-sm] [probability text-xs tabular-nums] [match dot]
```

- Role icon: `<RoleIcon role={r} size={20} style={{ color: ROLE_COLORS[r] }}>` — uses the per-role color from the palette
- Role name: `text-sm font-semibold` (primary role) or `text-sm font-normal` (arc roles), `colors.textPrimary`
- Probability: `text-xs tabular-nums` in `colors.textMuted` format `"34%"`
- Match dot: `w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-green-500 shrink-0` — appears only when the role also appears in the other column. Positioned to the right of the percentage.

**Match indicator:** A small filled green circle (2×2, `bg-green-500`). Brand green `#427c42` is already associated with "done/success" in the codebase (witness "Done" badge uses `text-emerald-600 bg-emerald-50`). Green for "match" reads naturally as alignment.

**Row height:** Approximately 28px per row (20px icon + 4px vertical padding per row).

**Row gap:** `gap-2` (0.5rem) between rows.

**ASCII mockup (desktop):**

```
╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║ ROLE COMPARISON                                                  ║
║ [SectionLabel gray]                                              ║
║ ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── ║
║ Card (shadow-sm p-5)                                             ║
║ ┌──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┐ ║
║ │ YOU SEE YOURSELF AS      │ WITNESSES SEE YOU AS             │ ║
║ │ [SectionLabel inline]    │ [SectionLabel inline]            │ ║
║ │                          │                                  │ ║
║ │ [🐬] Dolphin    34%  ●  │ [🐬] Dolphin    41%  ●         │ ║
║ │ [🦁] Wolf       21%     │ [🦉] Owl        22%             │ ║
║ │                          │                                  │ ║
║ │       ● = also seen      │                                  │ ║
║ │         by witnesses     │                                  │ ║
║ └──────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────┘ ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

**ASCII mockup (mobile — stacked):**

```
╔══════════════════════════════════╗
║ Card (shadow-sm p-5)            ║
║ ┌────────────────────────────┐  ║
║ │ YOU SEE YOURSELF AS        │  ║
║ │                            │  ║
║ │ [🐬] Dolphin    34%  ●    │  ║
║ │ [🦁] Wolf       21%        │  ║
║ ├────────────────────────────┤  ║
║ │ WITNESSES SEE YOU AS       │  ║
║ │                            │  ║
║ │ [🐬] Dolphin    41%  ●    │  ║
║ │ [🦉] Owl        22%        │  ║
║ └────────────────────────────┘  ║
╚══════════════════════════════════╝
```

**Typography detail:**
- Column sub-header: 12px Roboto SemiBold, uppercase, wide-spaced, gray-400
- Primary role name: 14px Roboto SemiBold, `colors.textPrimary` (#111111)
- Arc role names: 14px Roboto Regular, `colors.textMuted` (#666666)
- Probability: 12px Roboto, tabular numerals, `colors.textMuted`
- Match dot: 8px diameter (w-2 h-2), `bg-green-500` (Tailwind class, consistent with "Done" badge green)

**Icon color:** Role-specific hue from `ROLE_COLORS[r]`. This provides immediate visual identity — the Dolphin row is always red-tinted, the Eagle row always green, etc. It mirrors the existing `RoleProbabilityBars` pattern (currently using `colors.primary` for primary and `colors.arcBar` for arc — FM-R.2 replaces this with per-role color).

**Asymmetric case (e.g. 4 roles on self-side, 1 on witness-side):** The columns simply have different numbers of rows. No centering or padding is needed — the `gap-2 flex flex-col` list grows naturally. The shorter column just ends earlier.

---

### 5C. Surprises section

**Show condition:** Hidden entirely when `surprises.length === 0`. No "no surprises" message.

**Header text:** `"Surprises"` → i18n key `witnessResults.surprisesSection`
**Sub-header note:** `"Roles that appear on one side but not the other."` → i18n key `witnessResults.surprisesNote`

**Two types of surprise:**
- **Witness-only** (👁): Role appears in witness's relevant set but not self's. The witness sees something the user doesn't claim.
- **Self-only** (🪞): Role appears in self's relevant set but not witness's. The user claims something the witness doesn't see.

**Each surprise entry:**

```
[type icon] [RoleIcon 20px colored] [Role name]
            Defined by: adjective, adjective, adjective, adjective, adjective
```

- Type icon: text-sm `👁` or `🪞` (emoji, no i18n key needed — universal)
- Role icon: `<RoleIcon role={r} size={20} style={{ color: ROLE_COLORS[r] }}>`
- Role name: `text-sm font-semibold colors.textPrimary`
- "Defined by:" prefix: `text-xs colors.textMuted` + adjective list as plain comma-separated text
- Adjectives: the top 5 from `ROLE_TOP_ADJECTIVES[r]`, formatted as `en/ca` pair or just English (depending on language)

**Container:** `Card className="px-5 py-4"` (same as current blind spots Card)

**ASCII mockup:**

```
╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║ SURPRISES                                                    ║
║ [SectionLabel gray]                                          ║
║ Roles that appear on one side but not the other.            ║
║ [text-xs textMuted note]                                     ║
║ ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── ║
║ Card (px-5 py-4)                                            ║
║ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ ║
║ │ 👁  [🦊] Fox                                           │ ║
║ │      Defined by: critical, creative, curious,          │ ║
║ │      scattered, irritable                              │ ║
║ │                                                        │ ║
║ │ 🪞  [🐬] Dolphin                                       │ ║
║ │      Defined by: outgoing, enthusiastic, warm,         │ ║
║ │      empathetic, composed                              │ ║
║ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

**Entry layout (JSX structure):**
```
<div className="flex flex-col gap-3">
  {surprises.map(s => (
    <div key={s.role} className="flex items-start gap-3">
      <span className="text-base shrink-0 mt-0.5">{s.type === 'witnessOnly' ? '👁' : '🪞'}</span>
      <div>
        <div className="flex items-center gap-1.5 mb-0.5">
          <RoleIcon role={s.role} size={20} style={{ color: ROLE_COLORS[s.role] }} />
          <span className="text-sm font-semibold" style={{ color: colors.textPrimary }}>
            {t(`roles.${s.role}.name`)}
          </span>
        </div>
        <p className="text-xs leading-relaxed" style={{ color: colors.textMuted }}>
          {t('witnessResults.definedBy')}: {ROLE_TOP_ADJECTIVES[s.role].map(id => adj[id].en).join(', ')}
        </p>
      </div>
    </div>
  ))}
</div>
```

---

### 5D. Conditional rendering rules

| Condition | Side-by-side block | Surprises block |
|---|---|---|
| `!hasAnyWitness` | Hidden | Hidden |
| `hasAnyWitness && !hasEnoughWitnesses` | Hidden | Hidden |
| `hasEnoughWitnesses && surprises.length === 0` | Shown | Hidden |
| `hasEnoughWitnesses && surprises.length > 0` | Shown | Shown |

**Recommended threshold:** Both blocks gate on `hasEnoughWitnesses` (≥2 witnesses). One witness is insufficient to establish a pattern; the combined role is meaningful but not the comparison. This matches the existing gate on Sections 4 and 5.

---

### 5E. Edge cases

**No surprises (perfect alignment):** Section 5C is hidden. Section 5B shows with match dots on all entries.

**Only 1 relevant role per side:** Both columns render a single row. No "list" header needed — the rows speak for themselves.

**Asymmetric columns (e.g. 5 vs 1 roles):**
```
┌──────────────────────┬──────────────────────┐
│ YOU                  │ WITNESSES            │
│ [🐬] Dolphin  34% ● │ [🐬] Dolphin  58% ● │
│ [🦁] Wolf     21%   │                      │
│ [🦅] Eagle    18%   │                      │
│ [🐝] Bee      17%   │                      │
│ [🦊] Fox      16%   │                      │
└──────────────────────┴──────────────────────┘
```
The witness column simply ends after one row. The grid layout does not attempt to vertically align matching roles — doing so would require complex logic for diminishing benefit. **Decision pending.**

**Role icon color in probability bars:** `RoleProbabilityBars` currently passes no explicit color to `RoleIcon` (it inherits black/current). In FM-R.2, the comparison rows explicitly pass `ROLE_COLORS[r]` via `style`. `RoleProbabilityBars` is not changed in this release.

---

---

## 7. Implementation Plan for FM-R.2

### 7A. File-by-file change list (in implementation order)

**1. `src/utils/witness-scoring.js`**
- Add `ROLE_TOP_ADJECTIVES` exported constant (computed at module load from `WITNESS_ADJECTIVES` and `CENTROIDS`)
- Add `getRelevantRoles(probabilities, options?)` — returns `{role, probability}[]` sorted by probability descending, including only primary + arc roles above threshold
- Add `compareRoleViews(selfResult, witnessResult, options?)` — returns `{ selfRelevant, witnessRelevant, shared: Set<string>, surprises: { role, type: 'witnessOnly'|'selfOnly', probability }[] }`
- Delete `spearmanRho`, `spearmanLabel`, `computeRankComparison`, `detectDivergence`, `rankOf` (internal helper)
- **Estimated delta:** +60 lines, −120 lines = net −60 lines

**Estimated function signatures:**
```javascript
// Returns relevant roles (primary + arc) sorted by probability desc
export function getRelevantRoles(probabilities, { threshold = ARC_PROBABILITY_THRESHOLD } = {}) {
  const entries = Object.entries(probabilities)
    .filter(([, p]) => p > threshold)
    .sort(([, a], [, b]) => b - a)
  return entries.map(([role, probability]) => ({ role, probability }))
}

// Compares self vs witness relevant role sets
export function compareRoleViews(selfResult, witnessResult) {
  if (!witnessResult) return null
  const selfRelevant    = getRelevantRoles(selfResult.probabilities)
  const witnessRelevant = getRelevantRoles(witnessResult.probabilities)
  const selfSet         = new Set(selfRelevant.map(r => r.role))
  const witnessSet      = new Set(witnessRelevant.map(r => r.role))
  const shared          = new Set([...selfSet].filter(r => witnessSet.has(r)))
  const surprises = [
    ...witnessRelevant.filter(r => !selfSet.has(r.role)).map(r => ({ ...r, type: 'witnessOnly' })),
    ...selfRelevant.filter(r => !witnessSet.has(r.role)).map(r => ({ ...r, type: 'selfOnly' })),
  ].sort((a, b) => b.probability - a.probability)
  return { selfRelevant, witnessRelevant, shared, surprises }
}

// Computed at module load — top 5 adjective IDs per role
export const ROLE_TOP_ADJECTIVES = {
  R01: ['E+01','E+04','A+01','A+06','N-02'],
  R02: ['E+01','E+03','A-01','A-04','C+01'],
  R03: ['E-01','E-02','A+01','A+02','N-01'],
  R04: ['E-01','A-01','C+01','C+07','N-02'],
  R05: ['E+01','E+04','O+01','O+03','N-01'],
  R06: ['E+01','E+03','C+01','C+05','O-06'],
  R07: ['E-01','E-06','O+01','O+02','C-02'],
  R08: ['C+01','C+02','C+03','C+04','N-01'],
  R09: ['A+01','A+06','O+01','O+03','C+01'],
  R10: ['A+01','A+02','N-01','N-02','O-04'],
  R11: ['A-01','O+01','O+03','C-02','N+02'],
  R12: ['A-04','A-01','C+01','C+07','C+05'],
}
```

**2. `src/utils/__tests__/witness-scoring.test.js`**
- Delete: all tests in `describe('detectDivergence')`, `describe('spearmanRho')`, `describe('spearmanLabel')`, `describe('computeRankComparison')`
- Add: `describe('getRelevantRoles')` — threshold logic, primary always included, ordering
- Add: `describe('compareRoleViews')` — shared set, surprises, null witnessResult → returns null
- Update import line to remove deleted functions, add new ones
- **Estimated delta:** +30 lines, −100 lines = net −70 lines

**3. `src/components/report/ConvergenceMeter.jsx`**
- **DELETE entire file** (49 lines)

**4. `src/components/report/index.js`**
- Remove `ConvergenceMeter` export line

**5. `src/components/report/RoleComparisonView.jsx` (NEW FILE)**
- Side-by-side role comparison component
- Props: `selfResult`, `witnessResult`, `roleComparison` (output of `compareRoleViews`), `t`
- Internal: two-column grid, role rows, match dots
- Uses: `RoleIcon`, `ROLE_COLORS`, `Card`, `SectionLabel`, `colors`
- **Estimated size:** ~80 lines

**6. `src/components/report/SurprisesPanel.jsx` (NEW FILE)**
- Surprises list component
- Props: `surprises` (array from `compareRoleViews`), `t`
- Internal: maps over surprises, renders type icon + role icon + role name + adjective list
- Uses: `RoleIcon`, `ROLE_COLORS`, `ROLE_TOP_ADJECTIVES`, `WITNESS_ADJECTIVES`, `Card`, `SectionLabel`, `colors`
- **Estimated size:** ~60 lines

**7. `src/components/report/index.js`**
- Add exports for `RoleComparisonView` and `SurprisesPanel`

**8. `src/pages/FullMoonResultsPage.jsx`**
- Import changes: remove `ConvergenceMeter`, `spearmanRho`, `computeRankComparison`, `detectDivergence`; add `compareRoleViews`, `getRelevantRoles`; add `RoleComparisonView`, `SurprisesPanel`
- Remove derived values: `divergence`, `rho`, `rankComparison`; add `roleComparison = hasEnoughWitnesses ? compareRoleViews(selfRole, witnessRole) : null`
- Delete Section 4 (lines 282–290) and Section 5 (lines 292–334)
- Insert new section in their place:
```jsx
{hasEnoughWitnesses && roleComparison && (
  <>
    <section>
      <SectionLabel color="gray" className="mb-4">
        {t('witnessResults.roleViewSection')}
      </SectionLabel>
      <RoleComparisonView
        selfResult={selfRole}
        witnessResult={witnessRole}
        roleComparison={roleComparison}
        t={t}
      />
    </section>
    {roleComparison.surprises.length > 0 && (
      <section>
        <SectionLabel color="gray" className="mb-1">
          {t('witnessResults.surprisesSection')}
        </SectionLabel>
        <p className="text-xs mb-4" style={{ color: colors.textMuted }}>
          {t('witnessResults.surprisesNote')}
        </p>
        <SurprisesPanel surprises={roleComparison.surprises} t={t} />
      </section>
    )}
  </>
)}
```
- Also update Section 1 disclaimer gate if needed (currently `hasAnyWitness` — keep as-is)
- Also remove `BlindSpotsIcon` from import line (no longer rendered)
- **Estimated delta:** +20 lines, −60 lines = net −40 lines

**9. `src/locales/{en,ca,es,fr,de,da}.json`**
- Remove 17 old keys (listed in §6) × 6 files = 102 key deletions
- Add 9 new keys × 6 files = 54 translations
- **Estimated delta per file:** +9 lines, −17 lines = net −8 lines

### 7C. Translation strategy

Claude Code produces final translations directly to all 6 languages during FM-R.2. No draft markers. Catalan will be reviewed by the native speaker (product owner). The other 5 languages are best-effort machine-quality; any issues caught in product feedback.

---

## 8. Open Design Questions

### Q1: Section header wording
**Options:**
- A) `"How you and your witnesses see you"` — relational, explains the content
- B) `"Two perspectives"` — poetic, shorter
- C) `"Role comparison"` — clinical, explicit

**Proposed default:** Option A — it mirrors the exact cognitive frame the user needs.
**Decision: PENDING**

### Q2: Match indicator style
**Options:**
- A) Small filled green circle (`w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-green-500`) at end of row — minimal, consistent with dot patterns elsewhere (FacetAccordion domain dot)
- B) Entire row highlighted with a very subtle green-50 background when matched
- C) No indicator — let visual position of matching roles across columns speak for itself

**Proposed default:** Option A — the dot is the smallest possible signal that doesn't clutter. Option C risks users missing the alignment entirely on asymmetric layouts.
**Decision: PENDING**

### Q3: Mobile breakpoint for side-by-side → stacked
**Options:**
- A) Stack at `< sm` (640px) — side-by-side from 640px up
- B) Stack at `< md` (768px) — always stacked on phone and small tablet, side-by-side only on ≥768px

**Proposed default:** Option A (`sm:grid-cols-2`) — consistent with RadarDataCard which uses `md:grid-cols-2`. However, with only 2 columns (vs 3 in RadarDataCard), `sm:grid-cols-2` may be comfortable even at 640px.
**Decision: PENDING**

### Q4: Role icon color in comparison rows
**Options:**
- A) Per-role color from `ROLE_COLORS[r]` — visual identity, rich, mirrors the 12-color system
- B) Neutral (black / `colors.textPrimary`) — simpler, no color noise
- C) Red for primary role, gray for arc roles — mirrors `RoleProbabilityBars`

**Proposed default:** Option A — the per-role palette exists precisely for this use case. Using it here connects the comparison view to the broader role identity system. The colors are already coordinated and non-clashing.
**Decision: PENDING**

### Q5: Animation
**Options:**
- A) No animation — appear statically on render (consistent with all other cards)
- B) Fade-in on mount (`opacity-0` → `opacity-100`, 300ms)

**Proposed default:** Option A — no other section animates; adding animation here would be inconsistent.
**Decision: PENDING**

### Q6: Surprises type label display
**Options:**
- A) Emoji only (👁 / 🪞) — universal, no translation needed
- B) Emoji + short text (`"Witness only"` / `"Self only"`) — accessible, verbose
- C) Colored dot only (blue for witness-only, yellow for self-only) — minimal

**Proposed default:** Option A — the emoji communicate direction intuitively and require no localization. Screenreader `aria-label` can be set from the i18n key.
**Decision: PENDING**

### Q7: Adjective language in "Defined by"
**Options:**
- A) Always use the current UI language for adjectives (e.g. Catalan users see "creatiu, curiós, ...")
- B) Always use English regardless of language

**Proposed default:** Option A — i18n is a first-class concern in this product. `ROLE_TOP_ADJECTIVES` stores IDs; the component resolves `adj[id][i18n.language] ?? adj[id].en` at render time.
**Decision: PENDING**

---

## 9. Risk Register

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|
| `witnessRole` is null when `hasEnoughWitnesses` is true (edge: witnesses completed but all scored neutral) | Low | High (TypeError) | `compareRoleViews` returns null if `witnessResult` is null; page gates on `roleComparison !== null` |
| All relevant roles are shared (no surprises) on most users | Medium | Low | Surprises section hidden when empty — no visual debt |
| `getRelevantRoles` returns empty array (all probs below threshold) | Very low | Medium | Guard: always include at least the primary role regardless of probability |
| Per-role colors too similar at small icon size (20px) | Low | Low | Test visually; fallback to current primary/arc pattern |
| Removing 17 old i18n keys while code still references them | High if not careful | High (blank UI) | Grep verification step before commit |
| `ROLE_TOP_ADJECTIVES` IDs reference adjectives that were redesigned in commit 7589e3d | None | None | The IDs used (E+01, E+03, E+04, A+01, etc.) all exist in the current corpus |

---

*End of FM-R.1 audit report.*
*Next step: FM-R.2 implementation. Await decisions on §8 open questions before branching.*
